explanation: This command disables real-time monitoring using the Set-MpPreference PowerShell cmdlet, which is directly aligned with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command disables IOAV protection using the numeric equivalent of $true, which is also covered by the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command disables behavior monitoring, another Defender feature targeted by the Sigma rule.
This command disables the block-at-first-seen feature, which is also included in the Sigma rule's detection scope.
This command disables catch-up full scan, which is part of the Defender configuration monitored by the Sigma rule.
This command disables catch-up quick scan, another Defender feature included in the Sigma rule.
This command disables archive scanning, which is also covered by the Sigma rule.
This command disables real-time monitoring and pipes the output to the clipboard, using a common command-line technique that does not alter the core behavior.
This command disables real-time monitoring while hiding the PowerShell window, which is a common obfuscation technique but does not change the core behavior.
This command disables real-time monitoring while bypassing execution policy restrictions, which is a common administrative technique.
